data.topic: success always failure skipped job status
data.content:
### Conditionally Run Dependent Jobs in GitHub Actions with `always()`

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/write-workflows/choose-what-workflows-do/use-jobs

This example shows how to use the `always()` conditional expression with `jobs.<job_id>.if` to ensure a job runs regardless of the success or failure of its dependent jobs. `job3` will execute after `job1` and `job2` complete, even if `job1` or `job2` failed or were skipped. This provides more robust workflow execution for scenarios where a job should attempt to run under any circumstances.

```YAML
jobs:
  job1:
  job2:
    needs: job1
  job3:
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs: [job1, job2]
```

--------------------------------

### failure Status Check Function with Conditions

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/evaluate-expressions-in-workflows-and-actions

Returns true when any previous step in a job fails, or when any ancestor job fails in dependent job chains. Can be combined with additional conditions to execute specific steps on failure.

```yaml
steps:
  ...
  - name: Failing step
    id: demo
    run: exit 1
  - name: The job has failed
    if: ${{ failure() && steps.demo.conclusion == 'failure' }}
```

--------------------------------

### always - Unconditional step execution status function

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/expressions

Returns true and causes a step to execute regardless of previous step success or failure, including when a job is canceled. Best used for logging or cleanup tasks; avoid for tasks sensitive to critical failures as workflows may hang.

```yaml
if: ${{ always() }}
```

--------------------------------

### Handle Job Failures with Matrix Strategy in GitHub Actions

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/write-workflows/choose-what-workflows-do/run-job-variations

Configure failure handling using `fail-fast` and `continue-on-error` properties. The `fail-fast` property cancels all queued/in-progress jobs when a required job fails, while `continue-on-error` allows individual jobs to fail without affecting others. This example runs four jobs where experimental jobs can fail without cancelling the workflow.

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    continue-on-error: ${{ matrix.experimental }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: true
      matrix:
        version: [6, 7, 8]
        experimental: [false]
        include:
          - version: 9
            experimental: true
```

--------------------------------

### failure - Status check function for failed steps

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/expressions

Returns true when any previous step in a job fails, or when any ancestor job fails in a dependent job chain. Can be combined with additional conditions and step-specific conclusion checks to execute steps only on specific failures.

```yaml
steps:
  ...
  - name: The job has failed
    if: ${{ failure() }}
```

```yaml
steps:
  ...
  - name: Failing step
    id: demo
    run: exit 1
  - name: The demo step has failed
    if: ${{ failure() && steps.demo.conclusion == 'failure' }}
```

--------------------------------

### success - Status check function for successful steps

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/expressions

Returns true when all previous steps in a job have succeeded. Used as a conditional expression in if statements to determine if a step should execute. This is the default status check applied when no other status function is specified.

```yaml
steps:
  ...
  - name: The job has succeeded
    if: ${{ success() }}
```

--------------------------------

### Define Sequential Job Dependencies in GitHub Actions

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/write-workflows/choose-what-workflows-do/use-jobs

This example illustrates how to establish sequential dependencies between jobs in a GitHub Actions workflow using the `jobs.<job_id>.needs` keyword. `job2` depends on `job1`'s successful completion, and `job3` waits for both `job1` and `job2` to finish before starting. A failure or skip in an upstream job will prevent downstream dependent jobs from running unless explicitly handled.

```YAML
jobs:
  job1:
  job2:
    needs: job1
  job3:
    needs: [job1, job2]
```

--------------------------------

### success() Function

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/expressions

Returns `true` if all preceding steps in the current job have completed successfully. This is the default status check if no other function is specified in an 'if' conditional.

```APIDOC
## success()

### Description
Returns `true` if all preceding steps in the current job have completed successfully. This function is automatically applied as the default status check if no other status check function is explicitly used in an `if` conditional.

### Method
FUNCTION

### Parameters
N/A

### Example Usage
```yaml
steps:
  - name: Step 1
    run: echo "Running step 1"
  - name: The job has succeeded
    if: ${{ success() }}
    run: echo "All previous steps succeeded!"
```

### Returns
(boolean) - `true` if all previous steps succeeded, `false` otherwise.
```

--------------------------------

### always() Function

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/expressions

Causes a step to always execute, regardless of the success or failure of previous steps, and even if the workflow run has been canceled. Use with caution to avoid hanging workflows on critical failures.

```APIDOC
## always()

### Description
Returns `true` and causes the step to always execute, even if previous steps failed or the workflow was canceled. This is best used at the step level or for tasks that must run irrespective of job outcomes, such as sending logs.

### Method
FUNCTION

### Parameters
N/A

### Example Usage
```yaml
steps:
  - name: Critical step that might fail
    run: exit 1
  - name: This step always runs
    if: ${{ always() }}
    run: echo "This message will always appear, even if the previous step failed or the job was cancelled."
```

### Returns
(boolean) - Always `true`.
```

--------------------------------

### failure() Function

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/expressions

Returns `true` if any previous step in the current job has failed. For dependent jobs, it also returns `true` if any ancestor job in the dependency chain has failed.

```APIDOC
## failure()

### Description
Returns `true` if any previous step within the current job fails. If there is a chain of dependent jobs, it will also return `true` if any ancestor job fails. This function overrides the default `success()` check.

### Method
FUNCTION

### Parameters
N/A

### Example Usage
```yaml
steps:
  - name: Step that is expected to fail
    id: failing_step
    run: exit 1
  - name: React to failure
    if: ${{ failure() }}
    run: echo "A previous step failed!"
  - name: React to specific step failure
    if: ${{ failure() && steps.failing_step.conclusion == 'failure' }}
    run: echo "The 'failing_step' specifically failed."
```

### Returns
(boolean) - `true` if any previous step or ancestor job failed, `false` otherwise.
```

--------------------------------

### Control GitHub Actions Job Execution with Repository Condition (YAML)

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/write-workflows/choose-when-workflows-run/control-jobs-with-conditions

This YAML configuration for a GitHub Actions workflow demonstrates how to use the `if` conditional to ensure a job (`production-deploy`) only runs if the repository name matches 'octo-org/octo-repo-prod'. If the condition is false, the job is skipped, but reported as 'Success'.

```yaml
name: example-workflow
on: [push]
jobs:
  production-deploy:
    if: ${{ github.repository == 'octo-org/octo-repo-prod' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '14'
      - run: npm install -g bats

```

--------------------------------

### Configure GitHub Actions Job Failure Handling with fail-fast and continue-on-error

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/automating-your-workflow-with-github-actions/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

This configuration demonstrates how `fail-fast` and `continue-on-error` interact within a GitHub Actions matrix strategy. If any non-experimental job (where `continue-on-error` is false) fails, all other in-progress and queued jobs are cancelled. If an experimental job (with `continue-on-error: true`) fails, other jobs proceed unaffected.

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    continue-on-error: ${{ matrix.experimental }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: true
      matrix:
        version: [6, 7, 8]
        experimental: [false]
        include:
          - version: 9
            experimental: true

```

--------------------------------

### Run GitHub Action Job Conditionally on Commit Status State

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/events-that-trigger-workflows

This example defines a GitHub Actions workflow that triggers on a commit `status` change. It includes a job, `if_error_or_failure`, which only executes if the new commit status is `error` or `failure`, demonstrating conditional execution based on the `github.event.state` context.

```yaml
on:
  status
jobs:
  if_error_or_failure:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: >-
      github.event.state == 'error' ||
      github.event.state == 'failure'
    steps:
      - env:
          DESCRIPTION: ${{ github.event.description }}
        run: |
          echo The status is error or failed: $DESCRIPTION
```

--------------------------------

### Check workflow run exit status with GitHub CLI

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/monitor-workflows/view-workflow-run-history

This command views a workflow run and exits with a non-zero status if the run failed, useful for scripting. The example demonstrates chaining commands based on the run's success or failure.

```bash
gh run view 0451 --exit-status && echo "run pending or passed"
```

--------------------------------

### Allow Specific GitHub Actions Matrix Jobs to Fail Without Workflow Failure

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/automating-your-workflow-with-github-actions/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

This example shows how to prevent a workflow run from failing when a specific job within a matrix strategy fails. By setting `continue-on-error: true` for an experimental job (e.g., `node: 15`) and `fail-fast: false` for the overall strategy, only that specific job can fail without causing the entire workflow run to fail.

```yaml
runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
continue-on-error: ${{ matrix.experimental }}
strategy:
  fail-fast: false
  matrix:
    node: [13, 14]
    os: [macos-latest, ubuntu-latest]
    experimental: [false]
    include:
      - node: 15
        os: ubuntu-latest
        experimental: true

```

--------------------------------

### Conditional Job Execution with Repository Check

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Configure a job to run only on a specific repository within an organization using the `if` conditional statement. This example demonstrates how to skip job execution when the repository conditions are not met.

```APIDOC
## Conditional Job Execution

### Description
Execute a job conditionally based on the repository name and organization using the `if` keyword.

### Workflow Configuration

#### Condition Syntax
```yaml
if: github.repository == 'org-name/repo-name'
```

#### Parameters
- **github.repository** (string) - Required - The repository name in the format `organization/repository`
- **condition operator** (string) - Required - Comparison operator (e.g., `==`)

#### Example Workflow
```yaml
name: example-workflow
on: [push]
jobs:
  production-deploy:
    if: github.repository == 'octo-org/octo-repo-prod'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '14'
      - run: npm install -g bats
```

### Behavior
- Job runs only if the condition evaluates to `true`
- Job is marked as _skipped_ if the condition evaluates to `false`
- Useful for controlling deployment workflows across multiple repositories
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions: Step conditional using 'failure()' status function

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/creating-actions/metadata-syntax-for-github-actions

Shows how to use the 'failure()' status check function to run a step only when a preceding step in a composite action has failed. This is useful for implementing backup or cleanup procedures.

```yaml
steps:
  - name: My first step
    uses: octo-org/action-name@main
  - name: My backup step
    if: ${{ failure() }}
    uses: actions/heroku@1.0.0

```

--------------------------------

### Upload Pester Test Results as Artifacts in GitHub Actions

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/tutorials/build-and-test-code/powershell

This GitHub Actions workflow executes Pester tests and uploads their XML results as a workflow artifact using the `actions/upload-artifact` action. The job is configured to proceed even if tests fail (using `if: ${{ always() }}`), ensuring that test results are always available for review. This is particularly useful for debugging or reviewing test outcomes regardless of success or failure.

```yaml
name: Upload artifact from Ubuntu

on: [push]

jobs:
  upload-pester-results:
    name: Run Pester and upload results
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
      - name: Test with Pester
        shell: pwsh
        run: Invoke-Pester Unit.Tests.ps1 -Passthru | Export-CliXml -Path Unit.Tests.xml
      - name: Upload test results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ubuntu-Unit-Tests
          path: Unit.Tests.xml
    if: ${{ always() }}
```

--------------------------------

### Conditional step execution using status check functions in GitHub Actions

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/metadata-syntax

Demonstrates using the `failure()` status check function to run a backup step only when the previous step fails. This is useful for implementing fallback or recovery actions in composite workflows.

```yaml
steps:
  - name: My first step
    uses: octo-org/action-name@main
  - name: My backup step
    if: ${{ failure() }}
    uses: actions/heroku@1.0.0
```

--------------------------------

### Make job dependent on another job in GitHub Actions

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-jobs/defining-outputs-for-jobs

Creates a job dependency using the jobs.<job_id>.needs syntax to ensure a job runs only after another job completes successfully. This establishes the relationship between jobs for output passing.

```yaml
jobs:
  # Assume job1 is defined as above
  job2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: job1
```

--------------------------------

### Job Step Conditional Execution

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/contexts-reference

Defines available contexts and functions for jobs.<job_id>.steps.if conditional statements. Supports evaluation functions for job status conditions.

```APIDOC
## Workflow Key: jobs.<job_id>.steps.if

### Description
Configuration point for conditional execution of steps based on job status and context evaluation.

### Available Contexts
- **github** - GitHub context information
- **needs** - Outputs from dependent jobs
- **strategy** - Job strategy configuration
- **matrix** - Matrix build configuration
- **job** - Current job information
- **runner** - Runner information
- **env** - Environment variables
- **vars** - Repository variables
- **steps** - Previous step outputs
- **inputs** - Workflow inputs

### Available Special Functions
- **always()** - Always run step regardless of status
- **cancelled()** - Run if workflow was cancelled
- **success()** - Run if all previous steps succeeded
- **failure()** - Run if any previous step failed
- **hashFiles()** - Generate hash of file contents

### Usage Example
```yaml
steps:
  - name: Conditional step
    if: ${{ success() && hashFiles('package-lock.json') != '' }}
    run: npm install
```

### Restrictions
The `secrets` context is NOT available in step conditionals.
```

--------------------------------

### Job Conditional Execution

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/contexts-reference

Defines available contexts and functions for jobs.<job_id>.if conditional statements. Enables job-level conditional execution with status evaluation functions.

```APIDOC
## Workflow Key: jobs.<job_id>.if

### Description
Configuration point for conditional execution of entire jobs based on status and context evaluation.

### Available Contexts
- **github** - GitHub context information
- **needs** - Outputs from dependent jobs
- **vars** - Repository variables
- **inputs** - Workflow inputs

### Available Special Functions
- **always()** - Always run job regardless of status
- **cancelled()** - Run if workflow was cancelled
- **success()** - Run if all previous jobs succeeded
- **failure()** - Run if any previous job failed

### Usage Example
```yaml
jobs:
  deploy:
    if: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && success() }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
```

### Restrictions
Limited context access compared to step-level conditions. The `secrets`, `strategy`, `matrix`, `env`, and `steps` contexts are NOT available at this workflow key.
```

--------------------------------

### `jobs.<job_id>.concurrency` Configuration

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/writing-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Ensures that only a single job or workflow using the same concurrency group will run at a time. It can also cancel currently running jobs in the same group.

```APIDOC
## `jobs.<job_id>.concurrency`

### Description
You can use `jobs.<job_id>.concurrency` to ensure that only a single job or workflow using the same concurrency group will run at a time. A concurrency group can be any string or expression. It can also cancel any currently running job or workflow in the same concurrency group.

### Method
Configuration

### Endpoint
`jobs.<job_id>.concurrency`

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **concurrency** (string or object) - Required - The concurrency group definition.
  - If string: The name of the concurrency group.
  - If object:
    - **group** (string) - Required - The concurrency group name. Can be any string or expression. Note: The concurrency group name is case insensitive.
    - **cancel-in-progress** (boolean or expression) - Optional - If `true`, cancels any currently running job or workflow in the same concurrency group. Can be an expression.

### Request Example
```yaml
# Example: Workflow-level concurrency
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
```
```yaml
# Example: Job-level concurrency
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  job-1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    concurrency:
      group: example-group
      cancel-in-progress: true
```

### Response
#### Success Response (N/A)
Configuration successful. No direct API response.
```

params.topic: success always failure skipped job status
